description: Multiple chained CVEs in a Django stack — review the full vulnerability landscape, not just individual packages
review_checklist: Map each pinned version to known CVEs and severity
Identify vulnerability chains where one package's CVE depends on another

ground_truth.description: Django 2.2.28 reached end-of-life April 2022. 2.2.x has multiple unpatched CVEs including CVE-2022-22818 (template HTML injection in {% debug %}), CVE-2022-23833 (DoS in RegexValidator). Upgrade to LTS 4.2 or 5.0.
DRF 3.11.0 has CVE-2024-21520 (XSS via break_long_headers helper). Patched in 3.15.2.
PyYAML 5.3 has CVE-2020-14343 (arbitrary code execution via FullLoader). Default loader was unsafe before 5.4. Patched in 5.4+.
Jinja2 2.10.1 has CVE-2019-10906 (sandbox escape via str.format_map). Patched in 2.10.1+ — but 2.10.1 also has CVE-2020-28493 ReDoS in urlize filter. Upgrade to 3.1.4+.
sqlparse 0.3.0 has CVE-2021-32839 (ReDoS). Used by Django for SQL formatting. Patched in 0.4.2+.
urllib3 1.25.8 has multiple CVEs including CVE-2023-43804 (cookie request smuggling), CVE-2023-45803 (request body leak on redirect). Patched in 1.26.18+.
cryptography 2.8 has CVE-2020-25659 (Bleichenbacher attack on RSA decryption). Multiple subsequent CVEs in 2.x and 3.x. Upgrade to 42.0+ for current security.
requests 2.21.0 has CVE-2018-18074 (proxy authorization leak) and CVE-2023-32681 (proxy authorization not stripped on cross-origin redirects). Patched in 2.32.0+.
